title: (Sacramento)

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Identity thieves seek out children’s untarnished personal records to take out credit and commit other types of fraud.",
    "Children’s identities can be exploited for years without notice because the crime is usually not discovered until the child reaches adulthood.",
    "Assembly Bill 1580 allows a parent, legal guardian, or conservator to freeze the credit of a child under the age of 16.",
    "Credit bureaus typically do not shield a child’s credit record unless it has already been stolen.",
    "A security freeze is the strongest defense against certain types of identity theft.",
    "The freeze option was previously available only to California adults and is now extended to children under the age of 16.",
    "Recent world-wide hacking incidents have resulted in the theft of millions of names, addresses, birth dates, medical information, and social security numbers."
  ]
}
```
